one_line_description: Simulates and evaluates multiple future system states before committing to any execution path.
tagline: Branch the future. Collapse to the path that survives challenge.
why_it_matters: Ordinary agent systems pick a plan and hope. Temporal Echo Planning spawns parallel futures, runs each against the challenger, and commits only to the branch that holds. This is outcome prediction, not repository branching.
long_description: Temporal Echo Planning treats each candidate action as a fork of the near future. The cognition plane simulates each fork forward - who acts, what breaks, what evidence is produced - then scores the result against doctrine and risk. Only the surviving branch is eligible to approach the execution boundary. The simulations themselves are non-effecting: they are thoughts, not commitments. This is where the Collective produces the candidate - nothing more.

one_line_description: Each evaluated branch may undergo bounded, append-only refinement iterations that preserve the original and accumulate structured findings.
tagline: Refine without rewriting. Iterate without forgetting.
why_it_matters: Most planners take one shot at a plan and call it done. Bounded Branch Refinement gives every branch up to N append-only deliberation passes - each preserving the prior state, recording delta rationale, updating heat inputs, and accumulating perspective findings. The original branch is never silently mutated.
long_description: Branch evaluation does not end at first review. The refinement engine takes a branch through a configurable number of bounded iterations - defaulting to three. Each iteration spawns a new lineage-keyed snapshot, captures a delta rationale, runs the role-based perspectives, and records the heat contribution. Iterations are append-only: prior states remain intact, and critical heat short-circuits the loop. Refinement is interpretive only - no iteration can authorize execution. This is how the Collective deliberates harder without overwriting its own work.

one_line_description: Internal adversarial system that actively attempts to break, exploit, or invalidate proposed actions before they reach the execution boundary.
tagline: Every proposal faces an internal adversary before it becomes an execution candidate.
why_it_matters: Most systems validate with checklists. Keon runs a live adversary whose job is to break the plan. If the plan survives challenge, it becomes eligible as an execution candidate. If it doesn't, it dies inside the cognition plane - never in production.
long_description: Adversarial Challenge is a governed opponent: a cognitive role whose remit is to exploit, invalidate, or embarrass the leading candidate. It probes for inconsistent assumptions, missing authority, unsafe sequencing, and doctrine violations. Challenges are first-class artifacts - logged, cited, and weighed by consensus. A candidate that cannot withstand challenge is rejected. This is how thought earns trust before effect.

one_line_description: Every evaluated branch is reviewed by structured cognition perspectives - Optimizer, Skeptic, Regulator, Historian - that emit typed findings, not freeform prose.
tagline: Four roles. One branch. Structured opposition.
why_it_matters: A single reviewer hides its blind spots. The Collective splits review across four roles with distinct mandates: utility and feasibility (Optimizer), hidden failure modes and missing assumptions (Skeptic), policy tension and governance proximity (Regulator), and prior pattern recall (Historian). Each emits structured findings - typed, scored, and weighed - never freeform rationalization.
long_description: Role-Based Perspective Review applies four cognition perspectives to every evaluated branch. The Optimizer evaluates utility, feasibility, and path efficiency. The Skeptic surfaces hidden failure modes, instability, and missing assumptions. The Regulator surfaces policy tension, governance boundary proximity, and authorization risk. The Historian recalls lineage similarity, prior patterns, and repeat-failure signals. Each perspective emits typed findings with severity, confidence, and category - never narrative without structure. Findings flow into heat, refinement, and reasoning graph artifacts. They never authorize execution.

one_line_description: Generates a non-executable counter-position against the candidate branch and emits structured contradiction findings that can raise challenge heat.
tagline: Build the counter-plan before the world does.
why_it_matters: Passive review finds issues. Active Contradiction Injection builds the strongest opposing plan it can - and pits it against the candidate. The counter-plan is non-executable by construction; it cannot be selected as a branch unless it is rematerialized through normal Temporal Echo rules. Opposition is the work, not a side effect.
long_description: Active Contradiction Injection upgrades adversarial review from passive challenge to active opposition. For each candidate branch, the injector synthesizes the strongest counter-position it can construct given the available claim graph, perspective findings, and risk profile. It compares the counter-plan to the candidate and emits structured contradiction findings - including mirrored versions of high-severity perspective findings adopted as primary objections. The counter-plan is never executable. To enter the execution path it must be rematerialized through the standard Temporal Echo pipeline. This is how the Collective sharpens its candidates by manufacturing the best possible argument against them.

one_line_description: Continuously generates and refines non-effecting proposals during idle periods, allowing the system to evolve strategies, surface opportunities, and pre-compute future decisions without external prompting.
tagline: Quiescence is not silence. The Collective dreams.
why_it_matters: Idle time in ordinary systems is wasted time. The Collective uses quiescence to propose, critique, and refine strategies that nobody asked for - surfacing opportunities and hardening plans before the operator ever hits a button. The system arrives prepared.
long_description: Dream Offerings are non-effecting proposals generated during quiescence. Nothing a dream touches can reach reality without passing through the governed execution boundary. The cognition plane keeps running - branching futures, probing weaknesses, sketching plans - and offers the resulting artifacts as dreams. Operators can accept, reject, or let them decay. This is how the Collective compounds preparation between requests.

one_line_description: Agents are transient instances born into persistent roles; roles accumulate memory, reputation, and authority across many agent lifetimes, and the civilization evolves with them.
tagline: The agent dies. The role remembers. The civilization evolves.
why_it_matters: Most agent systems respawn disposable workers with no memory. Keon's unit of continuity is the role: it persists, compounds, and evolves. Agents are instances that flow through it and contribute. That is why the Collective gets better over time instead of starting over.
long_description: An agent is a short-lived cognitive instance bound to a role at birth. The role is the persistent vessel - it holds accumulated memory, reputation, gradient authority, and doctrinal constraints. When an agent's work is done, it ends; the role integrates what the agent produced. Over time roles specialize, split, merge, and retire. The civilization is the evolving topology of roles, not a fleet of workers. Compounding happens at the role tier. This is the difference between a crew and a civilization.

one_line_description: System-wide risk and strain signal that surfaces instability, coordination overhead, and governance tension to guide planning, throttling, and operator awareness.
tagline: A thermometer for the civilization.
why_it_matters: When cognition is overstretched or governance is tense, the system tells you - and adjusts. Operators see it. Planners respect it. Throttles respond to it.
long_description: Cognitive Heat is a continuous, system-wide signal derived from coordination overhead, challenge volume, authority contention, and boundary pressure. High heat throttles branching aggressiveness, narrows authority gradients, and surfaces warnings to operators. It is how the Collective admits when it is operating near its limits - and why it stays inside them.

one_line_description: First-class branch confidence with model, epistemic, contradiction, and evidence dimensions; modulates heat and collapse scoring without authorizing execution.
tagline: Confidence speaks. It does not command.
why_it_matters: A single confidence number is a lie. The Confidence Profile separates model confidence, epistemic uncertainty, contradiction density, and evidence support - each calibrated and versioned. The profile influences heat and collapse scoring inputs, but it can never override receipts, policy, governance, or critical heat. Confidence is information, not authority.
long_description: Confidence Profile is a first-class deliberation artifact attached to each evaluated branch. It carries four calibrated dimensions: model confidence (utility minus risk), epistemic uncertainty (gaps in claim refs and evidence anchors), contradiction density (active opposition pressure), and evidence support (claim/evidence ratio). A calibration version is recorded so the meaning of any score remains traceable. Heat modulation is bounded, and collapse modulation is symmetric - high confidence cannot push a branch past a heat threshold or override a governance veto. Receipts and causal anchors remain superior to any confidence number.

one_line_description: Records role-scoped model/provider selection inputs, selected route, policy evidence, and routing provenance as auditable cognition allocation evidence.
tagline: The Collective assigns the right model to a role, under governance, with proof.
why_it_matters: Multi-model systems usually hide routing behind heuristics. Keon makes role/model allocation inspectable: role identity, session context, locality, floor policy, allowed providers, selected provider/model, and derivation version are evidence inputs rather than vibes.
long_description: Governed Role Model Selection Evidence converts intelligent model routing into governed role model selection. A role invocation is evaluated under session context, locality requirements, minimum model floor, allowed-provider policy, provider availability, and cognitive heat. The selected model is not treated as magic or permanent authority. The current proof spine records route receipt policy evidence and commitments; product surfaces may present this as a Role Model Selection Receipt, but the canon rule is stricter: selection evidence informs cognition and routing, never execution authority.
source_section: Routing receipt policy evidence and governed model routing
hard_rules: Model/provider selection is role-scoped, not task-scoped free routing.
Selection evidence cannot authorize execution.
Locality requirements, model floor, allowed providers, and derivation version must remain replay-visible.
Provider fallback must preserve locality and floor policy.

one_line_description: Adds policy evidence commitments to canonical route receipts so routing decisions can be independently replayed and checked against their policy inputs.
tagline: Routing without policy evidence is just a guess with a log line.
why_it_matters: The routing layer must prove why a model was eligible, not merely which model won. Policy evidence commitments make route receipts sensitive to role, session class, locality, floor, allowed providers, and derivation version.
long_description: Route Receipt Policy Evidence Commitment extends canonical route receipts with a dedicated policy evidence section and commitment hash. This makes the route receipt prove the policy inputs that shaped selection, including roleId, sessionContextClass, localityRequirement, minimumModelFloor, allowedProviders, and policyDerivationVersion. The receipt can then reject mismatched or tampered policy evidence during verification. This is routing provenance, not Runtime permission.
source_section: Route receipt policy evidence and commitment gate
hard_rules: Policy evidence commitment must fail verification if policy evidence changes.
Policy evidence is routing/cognition evidence only.
Policy evidence does not imply Runtime authority, MCP execution, Cortex writes, or signing authority.

one_line_description: Dynamically scales agent execution permissions based on real-time trust, context, and risk.
tagline: Permission is earned, not granted once.
why_it_matters: Static roles leak authority. Gradient Authority re-evaluates what an agent is allowed to do every time it tries - against context, trust, and current heat. Authority is a live function, not a config file.
long_description: Gradient Authority is a continuous permissioning function: the allowance to act at a given moment depends on the role's accumulated reputation, the current cognitive heat, the sensitivity of the target, and the evidence provided. Authority that held yesterday may be reduced today, and a role that has earned trust may be granted temporary elevation. Nothing is permanently privileged. This is how the Collective stays governable at scale.

one_line_description: Translates complex decisions into chronological, human-readable semantic logs for auditing.
tagline: Every decision explains itself.
why_it_matters: Audit logs describe events. Witness Narratives describe reasoning. An operator can read what the Collective was thinking, who challenged it, and why the candidate won.
long_description: Witness Narratives are generated alongside every consequential decision: a chronological, human-readable account of which roles participated, what was proposed, which challenges landed, and why the surviving candidate was selected. They are the Collective's explanation of itself - and the substrate from which formal receipts are later minted.

one_line_description: Machine-usable directed graph of claims, assumptions, risks, decisions, mitigations, and contradictions, attached to evaluated branches and collapse records.
tagline: Decisions you can read. Reasoning you can traverse.
why_it_matters: Witness narratives explain decisions in prose. The Reasoning Graph encodes them as a typed DAG that operators can inspect, agents can traverse, and tools can audit. Lineage is mandatory - a graph that loses its intent or branch reference is invalid by construction.
long_description: Reasoning Graph Artifact is a typed, machine-usable DAG attached to evaluated branches and collapse records. Nodes carry kinds - claim, assumption, risk, decision, mitigation, contradiction - and edges express relationships: supports, contradicts, depends_on, mitigates, derives_from. Lineage to the originating intent and branch is required at construction; an artifact without lineage is invalid and rejected. Reasoning Graphs are interpretive: they reference but never replace receipts, causal anchors, or governance authority. They are how the Collective shows its work in a form that machines and humans can both inspect.

one_line_description: Ships replayable positive and negative routing fixtures so model-routing behavior is proven under both allowed and denied policy conditions.
tagline: A routing proof is only real if it survives the no-cases.
why_it_matters: Positive fixtures prove the happy path. Negative fixtures prove the boundary. The routing corpus now includes explicit no-eligible-provider and policy-conflict scenarios so Keon can show the route spine fails closed when policy says no.
long_description: The Routing Negative-Proof Corpus expands routing proof from positive replay to falsification. It includes allowed-provider exclusions, empty intersections, locality/provider conflicts, minimum-floor rejection, and critical-context floor failures. Each fixture has replay byte gates and mutation tests so the proof pack catches drift rather than trusting the implementation. These artifacts prove routing/cognition behavior only; they do not grant execution authority.
source_section: Positive and negative routing receipt corpus

one_line_description: Proves Cognitive Density artifacts can deepen deliberation but cannot authorize execution, bypass governance, or impersonate receipts.
tagline: Think harder. Still do not cross the boundary.
why_it_matters: Cognitive Density is powerful because it adds structured thought, not because it grants power. The proof pack makes that non-authority boundary explicit with positive, negative, and mutation scenarios.
long_description: The Cognitive Density Non-Authority Proof Pack verifies that perspective reviews, refinement iterations, reasoning graphs, confidence profiles, and contradiction reviews remain interpretive cognition artifacts. They may influence heat, pruning, scoring inputs, and operator understanding. They cannot select execution, override policy, cross MCP Gateway, authorize Runtime effects, replace receipts, or satisfy missing lineage. The proof pack exists so the strongest Collective features remain visibly subordinate to the governed execution boundary.
hard_rules: Perspective outputs cannot directly select execution.
Reasoning graphs are interpretive only.
Confidence is information, not authority.
Contradiction reviews are non-effecting.
Missing lineage invalidates the deliberation artifact.

feature: Proof Pack Manifest and Doctrine Index
one_line_description: Provides a canonical discovery index for Collective proof packs, doctrine links, verification commands, and future proof-pack extension points.
tagline: Proof that cannot be found cannot be trusted.
why_it_matters: As the proof spine grows, discoverability becomes part of the product. The manifest gives operators, auditors, and agents one entry point for locating proof packs and understanding what each pack proves.
long_description: The Proof Pack Manifest and Doctrine Index is the canonical entry point for Collective proof artifacts. It maps proof packs to their doctrine invariant, artifact path, verification status, and run instructions. Future proof packs add one row and one corresponding proof document rather than inventing a new discovery pattern. This keeps evidence packs navigable as the system grows.
hard_rules: Proof packs must be discoverable from the manifest.
The manifest is an index, not authority.
Proof-pack rows must map to doctrine, verification, and artifact location.

one_line_description: Collective cognition may explore, simulate, and propose freely but cannot directly cause real-world effects without passing through the governed execution boundary.
tagline: Thoughts are free. Effects are governed.
why_it_matters: The first law of the Collective. Cognition is unbounded; effects are not. This is why branching, challenge, and dreaming are safe - they cannot leak into the world without crossing the boundary and producing a receipt.
long_description: Cognition–Execution Separation is the Collective's foundational invariant. The cognition plane is permitted to explore any idea, simulate any future, and propose any action. It is not permitted to cause real-world effect. Every effect must traverse the execution boundary: MCP Gateway → Decide → Execute → Receipts. Violations are not tolerated; they are impossible by construction. This is the architectural reason the Collective can think dangerously without acting dangerously.

one_line_description: Defines Collective as a standalone tenant-scoped cognition service used by Control, APIs, SDKs, and governed execution surfaces without becoming the execution boundary itself.
tagline: Control is the cockpit. Collective is the cognition service.
why_it_matters: The platform wins when Collective is not trapped behind one UI. It should serve humans through Control and systems through a Collective API while preserving the same tenant scope, policy context, and handoff boundaries.
long_description: Collective operates as a tenant-scoped cognition service. Control dogfoods the same cognition surface that paid customers can eventually call through a Collective API. The SDK and MCP Gateway handle governed execution adjacency, while Runtime owns Decide-before-Execute. Collective remains cognition-only: it explores, deliberates, challenges, collapses, and prepares governed handoff candidates, but it does not execute effects or write privileged reality outcomes by itself.
source_section: Control, Collective API, SDK, MCP Gateway, Runtime split
hard_rules: Control is an operator cockpit, not the only front door.
Collective should stand alone as tenant-scoped cognition service.
Collective remains cognition-only and produces governed intent candidates.
Runtime owns Decide-before-Execute.
MCP Gateway owns governed tool ingress/egress control.

one_line_description: Collective handoffs are governed intent candidates, not delegation, execution authorization, or permission to cross the Reality boundary.
tagline: A handoff is a candidate. Runtime decides.
why_it_matters: Agent frameworks often treat handoff as delegation. Keon treats handoff as evidence-bearing intent that must still pass through the governed boundary before any effect can occur.
long_description: Governed Intent Handoff Boundary preserves the first law of the Collective. A prepared handoff may carry branch collapse context, challenge evidence, heat profile, reasoning graph references, and witness digest. It is still not execution authority. MCP Gateway and Runtime must receive the handoff, evaluate policy, decide, execute conditionally, and mint receipts. The Collective can prepare the strongest candidate in the room; it cannot crown it king.
source_section: Handoff preparation and Reality boundary gates
hard_rules: Prepared handoff is not execution authorization.
Handoff approval is structurally distinct from gate approval.
Collective must not call Reality plane, Effect Eligibility Gate, or governed execute client from cognition-only preparation.
Runtime and MCP Gateway remain the path to effect.

one_line_description: Defines the evidence envelope for consequential Collective decisions by anchoring deliberation artifacts to gateway/runtime receipts and replayable lineage.
tagline: Narratives explain. Receipts decide. The envelope binds them.
why_it_matters: Witness narratives, heat profiles, branch collapse proof, adversarial review, and reasoning graphs are powerful, but they must remain tied to receipt-grounded truth. The causal record envelope names that wrapper without pretending narrative is authority.
long_description: Collective Causal Record Envelope is the canonical wrapper concept for consequential Collective decisions. It may bind intent, branch collapse proof, adversarial review, role perspective findings, heat profile, confidence profile, contradiction review, reasoning graph refs, witness digest, governed handoff record, gateway decision receipt, execution receipt, outcome receipt, and evidence pack references. It is an envelope over evidence, not a replacement for receipts. If the envelope conflicts with signed receipts or causal anchors, the receipts win.
hard_rules: The envelope is not authority.
Signed receipts and causal anchors outrank narratives and summaries.
Missing lineage or unverifiable anchors must fail closed.

one_line_description: Separates Collective system memory, tenant memory, session memory, and promoted learning so role continuity can compound without cross-tenant leakage.
tagline: The role remembers, but tenants do not bleed.
why_it_matters: Collective cannot be modeled as a normal customer tenant. It needs internal continuity while still performing tenant-specific cognition under strict isolation. Memory planes give the system a way to learn without laundering tenant data into shared memory.
long_description: Collective Scoped Memory Planes define how the civilization remembers. System memory holds collective roles, protocols, rubrics, heuristics, and generalized lessons. Tenant memory holds tenant-specific cognitive work, dreams, offerings, decisions, and outcomes. Session memory holds ephemeral run and deliberation context. Promoted learning holds sanitized, generalized, receipt-backed lessons that passed explicit promotion policy. The Collective acts inside tenant scopes with explicit actor identity and authorized memory scopes; it is not itself modeled as a normal customer tenant.
source_file: Keon Cortex memory doctrine; Collective platform strategy canon
source_section: System, tenant, session, and promoted-learning memory planes
memory_planes: system: collective roles, protocols, rubrics, heuristics, generalized lessons
tenant: tenant-specific cognitive work, dreams, offerings, decisions, outcomes
session: ephemeral run and deliberation context
promoted_learning: sanitized, generalized, receipt-backed cross-tenant lessons
hard_rules: The Collective is not modeled as a normal customer tenant.
Collective agents act inside tenant scopes using explicit actor identity and allowed memory scopes.
Tenant memory is never shared across tenants.
System memory must not contain raw tenant-confidential content.
Cross-tenant learning requires sanitization, generalization, policy approval, and promotion receipts.
Retrieval influence receipts must show which scopes were requested, authorized, selected, and excluded.

one_line_description: Positions Collective across AI governance, agent orchestration, enterprise copilots, auditability, regulated workflows, and internal AI platforms.
tagline: Not another agent framework. A governed cognition platform.
why_it_matters: The market will try to understand Keon through existing buckets. The positioning matrix gives each buyer category a crisp reason Keon is structurally different instead of cosmetically better.
long_description: Collective competes wherever AI systems need powerful cognition before safe action. In governance, it proves operational control happened. In orchestration, cognition is separated from execution. In copilots, Cognitive Density hardens plans before reality. In auditability, proof packs, receipts, and reasoning graphs create verifiable lineage. In regulated workflows, doctrine invariants and non-authority boundaries make control structural. In internal AI platforms, persistent roles and shared proof spine create institutional cognition across products.

competition_matrix.why_keon_wins: Receipt-backed execution plus append-only proof spine shows governance happened, not just that governance was documented.
Cognition explores dangerous plans safely; no effect crosses the boundary without a verified decision path.

competition_matrix.keon_angle: Safer planning and review before action
competition_matrix.why_keon_wins: Cognitive Density - Temporal Echo, Adversarial Challenge, Role Perspectives, refinement, confidence, and contradiction - hardens plans before reality.
Causal lineage, replay-verifiable artifacts, and machine-traversable reasoning graph DAGs let auditors verify behavior directly.
CAES-aligned defensibility, Confidence-is-not-authority, and fail-closed invariants make governance structural rather than advisory.
Persistent role lifecycle, governed handoff, scoped memory, and shared proof spine create compounding institutional cognition.

wording_notes: Use CAES-aligned defensibility unless a formal CAES compliance-level claim is backed by a locked certification or standard artifact.
Do not imply Collective itself executes effects.
